title: [lora_l2.c:650-658] TX checks initialized/running but not deiniting
description:
Gap found: TX state machine checks incomplete.

Why this was missed: Round 1 found missing needs_reinit but not deiniting check.

The actual bug: In lichen_lora_l2_tx() lines 650-658, the function checks:
- !initialized -> return -ENODEV
- !running -> return -ENETDOWN

But does NOT check:
- deiniting flag
- aborted flag (Round 1 already noted this as project-LICHEN-tvfm.29)

If deinit() is in progress, the initialized flag is still 1 (cleared at line 540 in deinit), and running is 0 (cleared by stop before deinit). So !running check catches the deinit case.

Actually, deinit requires stop first, and stop sets running=0. So during deinit:
- initialized=1 (until line 540)
- running=0 (from stop)
- deiniting=1 (from line 490)

TX would fail at line 656: !running -> -ENETDOWN. This is correct.

The gap: if start() is called during deinit (between lines 490 and 540), start() has the deiniting check (line 358) and rejects. TX during this window fails with -ENETDOWN which is the right error.

Verified correct. Priority 4 - state machine analysis confirms correctness.
close_reason: Fixed: Added state coverage comment documenting that DEINITING case is handled by existing state != RUNNING check
